evals.name: add-with-known-repo
evals.prompt: Add `bat` (sharkdp/bat) to ~/.dot/tools.py for both macOS and Linux.
evals.expected_output: Queries `gh api repos/sharkdp/bat/releases/latest`, picks `aarch64-apple-darwin` for darwin and `x86_64-unknown-linux-musl` for linux (preferring musl over gnu), templates the version into the asset names, HEAD-checks both URLs, inserts a `Tool(...)` block alphabetically before `copilot`, runs `uv run update_shas.py`, shows git diff. Does NOT write `sha256` by hand and does NOT include `tag_prefix="v"` (default).

evals.name: add-linux-only-with-discovery
evals.prompt: Let's start managing the `dust` disk-usage tool — linux only.
evals.expected_output: Runs `gh search repos dust` to discover the canonical repo (bootandy/dust), confirms with the user, queries the latest release, picks only the LINUX_AMD64 asset, omits DARWIN_ARM64 from the assets dict, inserts the entry alphabetically, runs `uv run update_shas.py`.

evals.name: add-raw-binary
evals.prompt: Add `yq` from mikefarah/yq to my tools, both platforms.
evals.expected_output: Identifies that the release ships raw binaries (no archive extension), sets `is_raw_binary=True`, picks `yq_darwin_arm64` and `yq_linux_amd64` (or whatever the actual asset names are), does NOT set `extra_binaries` (model validator forbids the combination with raw binaries), inserts alphabetically, runs `uv run update_shas.py`.
